transcript.text: I can keep this a little bit concise for you so you can get off the This meeting is being recorded.
I would honestly appreciate that.
I think both Anketa and Mahindra are currently out of office.
Okay.
So if you can give the CVE updates to him over Slack, that would be really great.
Yeah, I think he had popped in and scanned the agenda and followed up already.
So I think we're good there.
I'll make sure if there's any other CVE updates from our ENG team to pass those on.
But it sounds like the code freeze is still in place.
So I figured I wanted to introduce Bailey.
So we'll get to that in a second.
And then we can kind of dive into some of the CS101 content.
Got it.
Hi, Bailey.
I'm so sorry.
I directly jumped into the agenda.
No, it's all good.
So quick update.
And I had shared in Slack as well.
Bailey is going to be joining as your engagement manager.
So she'll
be taking over all the day-to-day comms and looking at big picture strategy with you all.
She and I have spent quite a bit of time.
I've read her into a lot of what we have been working on and some key strategies that we have been looking into.
She's wonderful.
I will let her introduce herself a little bit more.
Hi.
It's really nice to meet you.
I'm excited to be joining the team and the account.
And Katya has done a great job catching me up on all the work you've done so far and some of the areas we're going to be focusing on going forward.
So just a little about my background.
I've worked in content across agencies and also in-house for quite a long time.
Most recently, I was running content at an AI services startup that was building bespoke AI products for mostly enterprise clients, a lot in regulated industries like finance and healthcare.
So that's sort of my background.
Background.
And so coming into this, I'm excited to catch up on all the work you've done so far and kind of hear more about where your focus is going forward.
And I'll be on Slack and I'll be sort of leading these meetings going forward.
But Katya and I have been working really closely together.
So hopefully the handoff will be as smooth as possible.
Yeah.
And I will, I'll still be in the background, able to support, like answer any questions.
I'm not totally disappearing.
So I want you all to know that as well.
One of the reasons that we thought Bailey would be a great fit, she's on a little bit earlier time zone.
And I know this meeting gets quite late for you all.
So something that you all can potentially chat about going forward is if, if we can bump this meeting up for you guys a little bit, so it's not quite so late.
That, that would be good.
So hi, Bailey.
Nice to meet you.
I will also quickly introduce myself.
So I am Vansi.
I'm handling the SEO and content side of things for specifically for Sentinel One CS.
you.
So all the content that you see, the articles that are getting published, and some of the SEO activities that are handled by me at the moment.
Okay, great.
Well, I'm looking forward to working closely together on all of that.
And yes, as I said, I'm on the Eastern time zone.
So if we want to move this up an hour and potentially even two hours, if that made it easier for your team, I'm happy to do that as well.
Great.
I think we can discuss about this one later on, but yes, surely that would be great.
Sounds great.
Perfect.
All right.
Just jump in to a few quick things.
So we've got five articles coming to you.
I'm really appreciative that you guys were able to approve some of those assignments that we sent over so quickly so we could keep that pipeline going.
So these are the five coming over to you this week.
They should be done polished by Friday.
And then for the revisions, I know you sent one that had basically...
We will be rerunning that one through our pipeline just to make sure that content is like really polished and accurate and all that good stuff.
So that revision in particular will come through next week.
I just wanted to flag that since typically we're able to get them back to you within the same week.
And also looking at all of our assignments, looks like we've got about three weeks of contract runway, which is nice going into the holidays.
So we've got a little bit of time and wiggle room there.
And one of the things coming up to keep poking at is some of that long tail keyword research.
We had discussed that as a possibility shortly before the holiday.

And so it's something that we are exploring to see if that is a good avenue for us to expand some of the CS101 content lanes rather than the really focused keywords we have been pulling so far, which was the primary content in the batch of assignments that I sent over earlier this week.
we a All Hold.
Okay.
Before I dig into the research update on some of those refreshes that we were looking into, I did want to check with you, Mansi, on your preferred word count, like sort of the depth of content.
One of the things I noticed in a lot of the revisions lately is that these articles are tending to get quite long, which isn't necessarily a bad thing.

But I wanted to make sure that like hitting that 3K word count isn't a red flag for you all, because we can potentially shorten the, you know, extent of information in each H2 if we still want those headers to be expansive in the different pieces that they're capturing.
So ideally, we are not very fixated on the word count, so for 3K is perfectly fine, but we, we prefer in the range between 2200 to 2500.
So that's, that's the ideal thing for us, but for some kind of, for some pieces where the TOC is, you know, a lengthy one.
If it exceeds three gates, it's not necessarily a problem for us.
Okay, great.
That's good to know.
With that in mind, a lot of the content that we develop like on a weekly basis, we typically aim for something between like $1,500 to $2,000 when we're delivering five a week.
So something that could help us ensure like we're giving, you know, really close read on all the accuracy pieces as we're working on like implementing those fact checkers and things for product dev.
If we're looking at articles where we're consistently at the like $2,500 to $3,000 range, I would suggest doing three articles instead of five.
It's just going to give us a little bit more space and room to give those really thorough reviews before we send them over to you.
So I wanted to flag that as something that we kind of have in our mind with these really expansive and in-depth articles.
For three articles, we'll be able to do, I think, three to four, 12 articles per month, right?
Okay.
I think I'll speak to Ankita about this, and I'll communicate over to you and Billy over Slack, if that's okay.
Yeah, yeah, that works.
I just wanted to flag that.
I know we didn't have a specific number in our scope of work outlined, and so it's one of the things that we look at as often, like, the word count that we are looking at delivering.
So, yeah, by all means, like, please, check with Ankita, and we can keep chatting about what that looks like.
That's perfect.
I'll take this up in tomorrow's stand-up call, and I'll let you know over Slack.
That's okay.
Okay, yeah.
As far as the refreshes go, I did some digging in with one of our directors kind of behind the scenes on areas.
So some context for as we're looking at refreshes and any sort of new content lane.
Really, what we're looking for are opportunities where...
We can get at least several months of runway for any new lane, in part because of the time it takes us to refine our pipelines and calibrate with you all on what those refreshes would look like.
Something that we noticed with CS101 is there's a ton of really recent content and looks like things have been updated fairly regularly, and it's already quite expansive.
Like most of the things we're looking at have really thorough table of contents and FAQs and a lot of those things that we would be able to kind of rapidly execute and implement at scale.

What we were noticing is there might be like a month of runway of content that we could refresh, which would really mean that your team and our team are spending a lot of time calibrating, and then we're potentially hitting another roadblock in the amount of content that we could be rapidly firing off to you guys within that refresh lane.
So at this point, our recommendation is probably actually not to do that refresh, in part, the original audit that was sent over had, you know, those 3000 articles that would
was conflated from how the URLs were pulled, and as we talked about last week, that's actually a much shorter list.

So I wanted to flag that, that after a little bit of digging, just so that both of our teams are using our time, and we're the best strategic partner for you in terms of delivering content really effectively at scale.
Because we also talked about not doing any refreshes regarding cannibalization since your team had done that.
It seems like that might be something good for us to put on the back burner.
Potentially, the cannibalization piece comes up later, like, we can definitely, like, bring that conversation back up.
I know Ankit mentioned that might be, like, every six months an activity that we could look at supporting.
But just wanted to flag that for you and your team, since I know that that was something that we were really working on the last couple weeks.
I know that makes sense.
Recently, we have been, I mean, I have been optimizing a lot of articles.
So, yeah.
Yeah, no, we were coming through and seeing like all the dates were pretty fresh.
Like it doesn't make sense for us to duplicate your work, right?
Cool.
So with that said, we are, like I mentioned, we are digging more into those long tail keywords to see if we can get a little bit more roadway there.
We do have that three weeks of content looking.
And with the CVE pipeline as well, like that is a quite extensive, that's sort of a great example of an area that we can execute rapidly at scale.
You know, 300K plus CVE entries.
We've got a ton of runway there to experiment and deliver just a massive amount of content for you guys on a weekly basis once that code freeze is lifted.

So it's something that we can all keep in mind, like, as we continue to look at opportunities and explore ways that we might be able to continue to like generate an editorial lane that is highly repeatable like that.
But yeah, just wanted to offer some context around there that we're thinking of.
I don't know.
I realized this is very wordy.
Do you have any questions about any of this or any other aspects around CS101 that you wanted to discuss today?
I just have a few updates for you.
So we went live with seven articles.
Awesome.
And then we, so I think the PMM have promised to give another five articles by, I think, 8th or 9th of December.
So before our next call, I think most chunk of our backlogs will be clear.
Awesome.
Yeah, that's.
Do you happen to have, oh, one other update that I made.
I realized that in this interface that I had sent over, not all of you had edit access.
I did actually update that.
So in this view, which is linked in the agenda, and I can link it directly to you if that would be helpful.
Um, you should be able to update these statuses here now.
Um.
So anything that was originally a PMM review, if you can just go ahead and send that to publication, and then we know to grab those links and update looker and all that good stuff.
Oh, perfect.
No, I can update this.
Amazing.
Thank you so much.
Yeah, sorry for that.
That oversight on my end.
I thought that all the permissions would work.
Airtable lied to me.
Cool.
Anything else on your mind that you want to discuss today, Mansi?
Okay.
So there was one article, if you can go through the, I incorporated Jeremy's comments, but there was one article called micro segment, if I'm not wrong.
So I think if, I mean, I've cleared his comments, but if you can just go through the comment, you can see them.
So he had a lot of feedback to give.
And since we are not, so since we are not in that space, so I have to.
You your H2 header also a bit.
So I do not want any misleading content to go in CSO 101.
It really backbites them.
So in spaces where we are not directly putting out our products or features, I do not want our H2 heading saying this with S1.
So micro segment with S1 will totally misguide the users.
So if you can tweak something like that.
Mm-hmm.
So I think this space...
Sorry, go ahead.
Yeah.
I'm so sorry.
In these spaces where we are directly not providing any product or features, let's not claim that S1 is in that space.
We can have CTAs.
We can have product mentions there.
But the H2 should be not promising that we are into that space.
That makes sense.
And I know that relates somewhat to our discussion and what we've got in the works to implement.
A fact checker for product placement.
And so we can incorporate that into the fact checker as well to make sure that those headers are accurately selecting where and where you guys do not have products that provide those direct services.
Yes, that's what we did for us.
Okay, perfect.
I had mentioned last week that we have a piece of content from you.
It says that you were the owner.
And I think it's a copy that I have.
I'm not sure where the original is that describes the products and services.
Can I send a version of that over in a Google Doc to you for you to check and see just in case there's a more updated version?
Because I know with your acquisitions of Observo and prompt injection, there might be some changes.
And we can use that to make sure that all of our fact checking is really up to date for current offerings that you all have.
Yeah.
I have made the document, I can make the changes, and I'll also have QC'd it once.
So it was reviewed by PMMs, and actually it's correct for the products that are in there, but we do have to add Observo and Prompt Engineering products to that.
Okay, I think the version I have access to, it's in our pipeline, which is distinct from probably the version that you had initially shared.
Do you mind sharing the link to the current doc that you have, so that we can make sure that our file is up to date?
I do have, but just give me time till tomorrow and revise it once again and add other products.
Perfect.
And not tomorrow, just give me time till Monday.
Monday sounds better.
Monday I can share it with you.
Yeah, yeah, no worries.
I just wanted to make sure that we're pulling all of the most up-to-date information.
That would be wonderful.
Awesome.
Well, thank you so much for flagging the micro-segmentation article in particular.
We can dig into that a little bit more.
Bailey or Mansi, do either of you have any last things?
I mean, got kind of a quick call today.

The only thing that I would flag is that I think next week on our call, especially if we have more of the full team here, it would be great to talk through a little bit more of that long-tail keyword research.

And for me, I think to get a better sense of, you know, what kind of topics your team is comfortable with, what kind of volume is kind of your threshold, just to inform that research as we kind of extend our runway.
So that's something I would love to kind of dig into a little bit more next week.
Makes sense.
Also, Mahindra would be POC for that.
So he handles all the topic approvals and the topics that have to be suggested.
So you can also see the comments for ejected and everything.
that's Mahindra, that's the POC for that.
Okay, perfect.
Awesome.
Well, thank you so much for your time, Mansi.
Really appreciate it.
Hope you're able to get some rest tonight.
Thank you so much.
I'll see you guys next week.
All right.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce new EM Bailey and sync on content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=35.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**EM Handoff:** Bailey Seybolt is the new Engagement Manager; Katya Luscomb will provide background support.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=52.0)
  - [**Content Cadence:** To improve quality, GrowthX proposed reducing the weekly article cadence from 5 to 3. Mansi will discuss with Ankita and confirm.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
  - [**Strategy Shift:** The CS101 refresh project is paused. Its limited runway (due to recent internal updates) makes it inefficient. GrowthX will now focus on long-tail keyword research to build a new content pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=464.0)
  - [**Product Accuracy:** GrowthX will add a fact-checker to prevent misleading claims (e.g., "Micro-segmentation with S1") in articles on topics where SentinelOne lacks a direct product.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)

## Topics

### EM Handoff & Scheduling

  - [Bailey Seybolt is the new Engagement Manager, taking over daily comms and strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=52.0)
      - [Background: Content lead at an AI services startup (enterprise clients in finance/healthcare).](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=92.0)
      - [Katya Luscomb will remain available for background support.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=130.0)
  - [The weekly sync time will be moved earlier to accommodate Bailey's Eastern Time Zone.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=149.0)

### Content Pipeline & Cadence

  - [**Deliverables:** 5 new articles are due this Friday.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=218.0)
  - [**Revisions:** One article requires a full rewrite and will be delivered next week, not this week.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)
  - [**Runway:** The current assignment pipeline provides \~3 weeks of content.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=260.0)
  - [**Proposed Cadence Change:**](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
      - [**Problem:** Articles are consistently long (2.5k–3k words), exceeding the ideal 2.2k–2.5k range.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=301.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This high word count strains the 5-article/week cadence, limiting thorough review time.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=367.0)
      - [**Proposal:** Reduce cadence to 3 articles/week to ensure higher quality and accuracy.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=367.0)
      - [**Decision:** Mansi will discuss with Ankita and confirm via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)

### Strategy Pivot: Pausing CS101 Refreshes

  - [**Decision:** Pause the CS101 content refresh project.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=464.0)
  - [**Rationale:**](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=464.0)
      - [The original audit list of 3,000 articles was inflated by a URL pull error; the actual list of refresh candidates is much shorter.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=530.0)
      - [SentinelOne's team has already completed recent, thorough optimizations on most relevant articles.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=592.0)
      - [The remaining runway is too short (\~1 month) to justify the time investment in calibrating a new pipeline.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=472.0)
  - [**New Focus:** GrowthX will now explore long-tail keyword research to build a new, scalable content lane.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=271.0)

### Product Accuracy & Fact-Checking

  - [**Problem:** Articles must not make misleading product claims.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
      - [**Example:** The "Micro-segmentation" article's H2 was edited to remove "with S1" because SentinelOne lacks a direct product in that space.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=752.0)
  - [**Solution:** GrowthX will add a product placement fact-checker to its workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=831.0)
  - [**Resource:** To ensure accuracy, GrowthX needs an updated product/services doc that includes recent acquisitions (Observo, Prompt Engineering).](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
      - [Mansi will update the doc and share by Monday.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)

### Project Admin

  - [**Airtable Permissions:** Edit access was granted, allowing SentinelOne to update article statuses (e.g., "PMM Review" → "Publication").](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=699.0)
  - [**Backlog:** 7 articles are live. 5 more are expected from PMMs by Dec 8–9, clearing most of the backlog.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
  - [**CVE Pipeline:** This remains a key opportunity for scalable content once the code freeze is lifted.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=619.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Mansi:**](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=413.0)
      - [Discuss the 3-article/week cadence proposal with Ankita and confirm via Slack.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=388.0)
      - [Update the product/services doc with Observo and Prompt Engineering info; share by Monday.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=862.0)
  - [**Bailey:**](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)
      - [Lead next week's discussion on long-tail keyword research.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)
      - [Sync with Mahindra (POC for topic approvals) on research findings.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=976.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=218.0)
      - [Add a product placement fact-checker to the content workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=831.0)
      - [Deliver 5 new articles by Friday.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=218.0)
      - [Deliver the revised article next week.](https://fathom.video/share/1ftz6L3arzvSTjK_z9dFX8gTv7N-k51b?tab=summary&timestamp=236.0)

action_items.description: Post CVE updates in Slack for Ankita + Mahindra
Discuss weekly output w/ Ankita; Slack Katya + Bailey decision
Update Airtable: set PMM-approved to Publication; GrowthX will pull links + update Looker
Revise 'Microsegmentation' per Jeremy; remove S1 claims from H2s
Update product/services doc w/ Observo + Prompt Engineering; QC + share link w/ Katya
